name: David Baker
description: American jazz composer, trombonist, professor of music and author (1931-2016)
abstract: David Nathaniel Baker Jr. (December 21, 1931 – March 26, 2016) was an American jazz composer, conductor, and musician from Indianapolis, as well as a professor of jazz studies at the Indiana University Jacobs School of Music.

interviews.doc_id: David N. Baker SI-NEA oral history (2)
interviews.title: David N. Baker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

works_created.t: Jazz Suite: Harlem, Saturday Night: 2. Harlem, Saturday Night
Sonata for Tuba and String Quartet
Singers of Songs, Weavers of Dreams

about_quotes.speaker: Larry Ridley
about_quotes.evidence: David Baker was there and he is a few years older than me but he’s like my big brother and he’s always looked out for me. In fact when I went to Yale University we were roommates.
about_quotes.doc_title: Larry Ridley interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 10, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Slide Hampton
about_quotes.evidence: Dave and I were very close, because he was born one year before me, and we both played trombone. So we were very close. We were practicing together a lot.
about_quotes.doc_title: Slide Hampton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Schuller Gunther
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee mentions David Baker as the creator of a jazz orchestra at Indiana University and later as the head of that department, but does not describe a direct personal, professional, or collaborative relationship between himself and Baker.
about_quotes.doc_title: Schuller Gunther — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Loren Schoenberg
about_quotes.evidence: And the conductors of that were Gunther Schuller and David Baker. And I was hired originally to be the contractor... I wound up becoming the substitute conductor, when Gunther or David couldn’t make it.
about_quotes.doc_title: Loren Schoenberg interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 14, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Cobb
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer mentions 'David Baker' as a recording engineer who did a lecture, but the interviewee (Jimmy Cobb) does not respond with any personal connection or memory regarding David Baker.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Cobb — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Diblasio
about_quotes.evidence: I taught at those camps, I see the way they teach, and I’m just stealing everything and I’m just dumping it on my students. ... I was talking to David Baker. Boy Dave was great.
